title: motion_mood_board.json — the design-first visual architecture (the motion score)
description: v1.0. The pre-script visual architecture that inverts the pipeline's creative logic: it is produced AFTER narrative_intent (the emotional blueprint) and BEFORE the script, and it governs BOTH Marlow's pacing AND Mason's motion design. Where narrative_intent says WHAT the viewer should feel, this says HOW the frame moves to make them feel it — translating the emotional arc into concrete HyperFrames directives Mason executes without interpretation. The closed vocabularies (effects, layouts, transitions, textures, emotions) MIRROR the engine's real HyperFrames vocabularies exactly (a cross-engine parity test guards lock-step) so every value maps to something Mason can render. Every field beyond schema_version is OPTIONAL and additionalProperties:true throughout, so the pipeline stays backward-compatible when the stage is absent and nothing breaks if a producer adds a field.

arc_phase.description: which of the five emotional-arc phases this beat belongs to (shared with narrative_intent)

primary_emotion.description: the closed emotion vocabulary the whole pipeline shares (mirrors narrative_intent)

pacing_profile.description: how this beat moves through time — maps to Marlow's sentence structure AND Mason's animation timing

effect.description: a HyperFrames per-scene effect token (the engine's real EFFECTS vocabulary, plus 'none')

transition.description: a HyperFrames assembly-seam transition token (the engine's real TRANSITIONS vocabulary)

layout_family.description: a HyperFrames layout token (the engine's real LAYOUTS vocabulary)

global_texture.description: the single global texture (the engine's real TEXTURES vocabulary, plus 'clean' for none)

global_tempo.description: the dominant rhythm of the whole video — affects default transition speed + scene duration

global_texture_justification.description: why this texture — must connect to the thematic anchor or emotional payload

dominant_motion_philosophy.description: one sentence that guides every motion decision (e.g. 'motion is punctuation, not decoration')

beat_map.description: one entry per beat in the emotional arc — the governing document for both Marlow and Mason

beat_id.description: unique identifier for this beat — links scenes to beats

scene_duration_target_sec.description: target total duration for all scenes in this beat — Marlow fits narration within it; Mason uses it for pacing

motion_parameter_overrides.description: concrete animation parameters that deviate from defaults, keyed by effect name (e.g. {'push-in': {'duration_sec': 1.8, 'easing': 'exponential-out'}})

visual_mood_ref.description: a single film scene / painting / photographic style that captures this beat's visual feeling (not a palette)

palette_emotional_overrides.description: optional per-beat color shifts from the global palette — used sparingly

accent_override.description: a single accent color overriding the global palette for this beat only

override_justification.description: why this color, for this beat, at this moment

signature_beat_placement.description: where the single #FFD000 highlighter moment lands

target_element.description: the exact word, number, or visual element the highlighter touches

justification.description: why this element, at this moment, deserves the signature
